rulings.Case Title: INVESTEK PROPERTIES VS ALTA MESA WIND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recall And Or Quash Writ Of Execution And Vacate Execution Of Levy And Liens
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 14, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT MARK TECHNOLOGIES CORP'S Motion To Recall And Or Quash Writ Of Execution And Vacate Execution Of Levy And Liens. Defendant's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recall And Or Quash Writ Of Execution And Vacate Execution Of Levy And Liens is GRANTED. Judgment entered by this court on July 20, 2011, provides plaintiffs with their monetary damages through a constructive trust. It is clear from the language of the Judgment that the court is limiting the source of the recovery to the post-judgment royalties. The Court lists the gross amount of the judgment as $1,238,379.00, then states that the Defendants are entitled to a credit of $882,267.00, then states that the net of Plaintiff's judgment after accounting for the defendants' credit is $460,391 in addition to $72,342.00 in pre-judgment interest. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SCOTT SMITH et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 14, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC.'S Motion. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 200-298 PORTOLA DRIVE HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION VS. COMAC INVESTMENTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 14, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT COMAC INVESTMENTS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's General Demurrer to the Complaint on the basis of the statute of limitations is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to plead facts showing that Defendant had to have been aware of the alleged construction defects and willfully ignored them. (See California Code of Civil Procedure § 337.15(f).) Plaintiff's boilerplate legal conclusion that the defects were the product of willful misconduct is not sufficient to save the claim from the 10-year Statute of Limitations. Plaintiff must plead facts, not legal conclusions. (See, e.g., Doe v. City of Los Angeles (2007) 42 Cal.4th 531, 551 at fn. 5 (complaint must plead facts, not legal conclusions, and it is insufficient to bring a claim outside a statute of limitations to merely "assert boilerplate allegations that defendants" had actual knowledge without alleging the facts which "lead[s] [the plaintiff] to believe that the allegations are true").) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: FLIGHTEN UP, LLC ET AL VS. ZOOMSYSTEMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 14, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT ZOOMSYSTEMS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. The Demurrer is overruled as to the 1st, 3rd, 4th, 5th Causes of Action. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 7th Cause of action. 22 of the complaint states "defendant ZoomSystems disclosed plaintiff's business idea¿ This conduct breached the non-disclosure agreement." Plaintiff states they have sustained damage due to the breach. Thus, P has properly alleged breach of contract. Thus, the demurrer is overruled as to the 1st Cause of Action. The elements that must be pleaded under a fraud cause of action are: "(a) misrepresentation (false representation, concealment or nondisclosure); "(b) knowledge of falsity (or 'scienter'); "(c) intent to defraud, i.e., to induce reliance; "(d) justifiable reliance; and "(e) resulting damage." [See Philipson & Simon v. Gulsvig (2007) 154 CA4th 347, 363.] Here, in the complaint D has alleged (1) misrepresentation; (2) knowledge of falsity; (3) intent to defraud; (4) reliance; and (5) resulting damage. (See Complaint 24, 48, 51.) Thus, the demurrer is overruled as to the 3rd Cause of Action. "Before a person can be charged with a fiduciary obligation, he must either knowingly undertake to act on behalf and for the benefit of another, or must enter into a relationship which imposes that undertaking as a matter of law." (Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 221, 197 Cal.Rptr. 783, 673 P.2d 660 (Children's Television ).) City of Hope Nat. Med. Ctr. v. Genentech, Inc., (2008) 43 Cal. 4th 375, 386. Here, Plaintiff has alleged it entrusted D with a confidential business plan and parties has executed a nondisclosure agreement to that effect, creating a fiduciary agreement between the parties. Thus, the demurrer is overruled as to the 4th Cause of Action. The Unfair Competition Law (UCL) is not limited to anticompetitive business practices. "Unfair competition" under the UCL includes "any unlawful, unfair or fraudulent" business practice. [Bus. & Prof.C. § 17200] Here, P has properly pled business practices forbidden by law, thus, the demurrer is overruled as to the 5th Cause of Action. Generally, conversion requires interference with tangible property, there can be no conversion of the goodwill of a business, or trade secrets. (Olschewski v. Hudson (1927) 87 C.A. 282, 286, 262 P. 43; see Rest.2d, Torts §242, Comment f; on equitable relief for improper use of trade secrets, see 13 Summary (10th), Equity, §§83, 89.; See 5 Witkin, Summary 10th (2005) Torts, § 702, p. 1025. Here, Plaintiff has not alleged Defendant interfered with tangible property, thus the demurrer is sustained as to the 7th Cause of Action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BRODY MD VS. KAMALA D HARRIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 14, 2015, Line 10, PLAINTIFF ROBERT BRODY, MARC CONANT, DONALD ABRAMS, ROBERT LINER, DANIEL SWANGARD, CHRISTIE WHITE, ANGELICA BLOOMQUIST'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. DENIED. Plaintiffs seek a preliminary injunction which "recognizes" that Penal Code section 401 does not apply to aid in dying. Plaintiffs assert that their claim is not moot under Bartling. (Bartling v. Superior Court (1984) 163 Cal. App. 3d 186, 197.) Bartling held that the terminally-ill patient's "death renders moot that portion of the petition which seeks an order compelling the superior court to grant the injunction sought." (Ibid.) Even if this court has considerable discretion because the restrictive federal doctrine of justiciability does not apply in state courts, White v. Davis (1975) 13 Cal.3d 757, 762, Bartling is apposite and establishes that plantiffs claim is nonjusticiable. Furthermore, even though state courts are not bound by Article III of the United States Constitution, Asarco Inc. v. Kadish (1989) 490 U.S. 605, 610, the California Supreme Court upholds the "majority rule depriving a derivative plaintiff of standing where, as here, his or her interest in the litigation is completely extinguished. " (Grosset v. Wenaas (2008) 42 Cal. 4th 1100, 1117.) Here, because the terminally ill patient's "own motion is rendered moot by her death." and plaintiffs are unsure whether their new patients "meet the criteria for aid in dying", even under California law it is unlikely that plaintiffs will prevail on the merits because the merits cannot even be reached. Even if this court could meet the merits of this case it is unlikely plaintiff would prevail. Penal Code Section 401 establishes that every person who deliberately aids another to commit suicide, is guilty of a felony. Statutory language should be given effect consistent with its plain meaning and in light of the aim of the legislature unless to do so would achieve an illogical result. (Radzanower v. Touche Ross & Co. (1976) 426 U.S. 148, 166.) Even if the legislature has not provided a statutory definition of suicide, California's ban against assisted suicide contains no exception to its prohibition. This court has received Plaintiffs' Sur-Reply. Plaintiffs do not establish that their case is justiciable on the basis that they are now "willing to explore" with new patients whether "they meet the criteria for aid in dying." Defendant Kamala D. Harris' Objection to Plaintiffs' Sur-Reply in Support of Motion for Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: GEORGE CHANG VS. TOM CHANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 14, 2015, Line 11, PLAINTIFF GEORGE CHANG'S Motion For Appointment Of Receiver. Granted. The Court has considered the possibility of less-intrusive alternatives to receivership. [See Gold v. Gold Realty (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 791,807.] On May 11, 2015, a restraining order was issued with a detailed list of conditions, in an attempt to avoid the need for more serious measures. When that approach failed, On July 13, 2015 the Court spent several hours working out a stipulation and order, signed by all parties, wherein they committed to a new, supplemented set of conditions for management of the corporation until the resolution of the case. The very next day, Defendants procured a domestic violence restraining order from San Mateo Superior Court which conflicts with the provisions of both the May 11, 2015 and July 14, 2015 orders of this Court. In this circumstance, the court Court finds a receivership is necessary to prevent the very real possibility of harm to the corporation and its shareholders before this case is resolved. (See Corporations Code § 1803.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
